column6: Target Workflow / LLM Spec (For the Dev / LLM)

Implement a custom dockable UI panel (QDockWidget) at the bottom of the main window to act as an AutoCAD-style command line. Capture global keyboard input automatically. Implement alias auto-completion (e.g., typing 'L' suggests Line). Pressing Spacebar or Enter executes. History log must display tool prompts and echo inputs.
Command 'UN'. Opens dialog. Sets document unit length (Decimal/Architectural) and precision. Updates FreeCAD internal unit preferences for the current document.

column5: Draft Scale requires complex UI interaction.
column6: Select objects, type 'SC' + Enter. Click Base Point. Type scale factor (e.g., '2' for double size) or drag mouse to scale dynamically. Execute on Enter.
Implement scaling logic tied to Viewport scale properties to dynamically adjust text and dimension sizes.

column5: Document bounds exist but aren't strict.
column6: Define invisible rectangular boundary. If limit checking is ON, reject point inputs outside this boundary.

column5: Draft Grid toggles but lacks quick scaling.
column6: Toggle visual grid dots/lines. Command 'GRID' prompts for grid spacing value.

column5: Grid snap exists in Draft tray.
column6: Toggle snapping cursor to grid points. Command 'SNAP' sets snap distance.

column5: Snaps exist but UI is different.
column6: Command 'OS'. Opens dialog to set running snaps (Endpoint, Midpoint, Intersection, Center, Perpendicular). Pressing 'F3' must globally toggle these running snaps on/off during any active command.
F11 toggle. When hovering over an active OSNAP point, display a tracking vector (dashed line) orthogonally from that point to align subsequent clicks.
Command 'UCS'. Prompt: Specify origin of UCS. Prompt: Specify point on X-axis. Prompt: Specify point on XY plane. Rotates internal Draft working plane to match.
Orient the 3D camera to look dead-on perpendicular to the current active UCS/Working Plane.
Command 'LA'. Opens a persistent Layer Properties pane. Buttons for: New Layer, Set Active. Columns for: Name, Visibility (eye icon), Color, Linetype, Lineweight.
Command 'LAYISO'. User selects objects. Script reads their layers, loops through all other layers in the document, and turns their visibility OFF.
Restores layer visibility states to exactly what they were immediately before the last LAYISO command was run.
Command 'LAYOFF'. User clicks an object in the viewport. The layer of that selected object is immediately hidden. Loops until ESC.
Command 'LAYON'. Instantly turns visibility ON for all layers in the document.

column5: No native lock concept in Draft.
column6: Selected layer becomes uneditable. Objects can be snapped to but not moved, deleted, or modified.
Similar to LAYOFF but prevents the layer from loading into memory during regens for performance optimization.
Command 'PR'. Opens a simplified Properties dock. When objects selected, show/edit: Layer, Color, Linetype. Changing a value instantly updates objects.
Ensure all geometry defaults to 'ByLayer' color, linetype, and line width handling.
Command 'MA'. Select source object. Read Layer/Color/Linetype. Select target objects. Target inherits exact properties. Loops until ESC.
Command 'LTYPE'. Open dialog to load custom line patterns (dashed, dotted, dash-dot) and assign global scale factors.

column5: Line width is just a float.
column6: Toggle global display of lineweights in the viewport on/off.
User types 'L' + Enter. Clicks point 1. Moves mouse (vector direction). If Ortho (F8) ON, lock vector to X or Y axis. User types distance (e.g., '5') + Enter. Command loops from last point until ESC.
Same behavior as Line, but creates a single continuous Polyline (Draft Wire) object instead of separate line segments.
User types 'REC' + Enter. Clicks first corner. Drag and click second corner, OR type relative coordinates like '@10,5' to define width and height. Inherits Active Layer.
User types 'C' + Enter. Clicks Center point. Drags mouse. User types Radius value + Enter. Object is drawn.
Command 'A'. 3-point arc. Clicks start point, clicks point on arc, clicks end point.
Command 'EL'. Clicks start of axis, end of axis, then distance to other axis.
Command 'POL'. Enter number of sides. Specify center. Choose Inscribed or Circumscribed. Specify radius.
Creates an infinite line. Specify point and direction. Used for layout construction.
Creates a line infinite in one direction. Specify start point and direction.
Create smooth NURBS curve passing through specified points. Press Enter to end.
Select objects, type 'M' + Enter. Click Base Point. Move mouse (respect Ortho). Type distance + Enter, OR click destination point. Objects are translated.
Select objects, type 'CO' + Enter. Click Base Point. Click destination point. Clone placed. Command LOOPS allowing multiple placements until ESC.
Select objects, type 'RO' + Enter. Click Base Point. Move mouse to define angle visually, or type angle (e.g., '45') + Enter. Positive is Counter-Clockwise.
Select objects, type 'MI' + Enter. Click Point 1 of mirror line. Click Point 2. Prompt: 'Erase source objects? Y/N'. Default N. Perform symmetry transformation.
Select objects. Pick source point 1, target point 1. Pick source point 2, target point 2. Scales and rotates object to align vectors.
Type 'TR' + Enter. Press Enter again to Select All as cutting edges. Click on segments of lines/polylines to delete them up to their nearest intersection. Loops until ESC.
Type 'EX' + Enter. Press Enter again to Select All as boundary edges. Click on line segments; they extend to nearest intersecting boundary. Loops until ESC.
Type 'F' + Enter. (Optional 'R' to change radius). Select first line, select second line. Trims/extends both to intersect and places arc of specified radius.
Type 'CHA' + Enter. Set distances. Select two lines. Trims and connects with an angled straight line.
Select multiple touching line/arc segments. Type 'J' + Enter. Converts them into a single continuous Polyline object.
Select Polyline or Block. Type 'X' + Enter. Converts Polyline into independent Line segments. Converts Block into constituent geometry.
Type 'O' + Enter. Prompt asks distance: type value + Enter. Select object. Click side of object where offset should appear. Loops until ESC.
Use Crossing Selection to grab nodes. Select base point, specify displacement. Moves selected nodes while keeping connected lines attached.
Command 'LEN'. Options: Delta, Percent, Total. Click object to change its total length without altering its vector angle.
Command 'AR'. Select Rectangular or Polar. Open dialog to input row/column count and spacing, or center point and item count.
F10 toggle. Forces tracking vectors at predefined degree increments (e.g., 0, 45, 90) relative to previous point.
Floating input boxes attached to the crosshair cursor that accept distance/angle values dynamically while drafting.
Apply persistent geometric rules (parallel, perpendicular, tangent) to 2D drafting objects outside the sketcher.
Apply driving dimensions to 2D drafting objects that force geometry to update when text changes.
Type 'T' + Enter. Click insertion point. A text entry box appears in 3D view. User types text, clicks outside or presses Ctrl+Enter to finalize.
Command 'STYLE'. Dialog to define named text styles linking a font file, text height, and width factor.
Type 'DLI' + Enter. Click Point 1. Click Point 2. Move mouse perpendicular to points to set offset distance of dimension line. Click to place.
Command 'DIMSTYLE'. Dialog to manage arrow sizes, text placement, extension line gaps, and primary unit precision for dimensions.
Dedicated aliases for placing dimensions parallel to angled lines, measuring angles between two lines, or displaying circle radii.
Click point to attach arrow, click point for landing line, type text annotation.
Type 'H' + Enter. Clicks internal point. Algorithm finds bounding edges, creates face, applies selected SVG/Hatch pattern.
Type 'B' + Enter. Dialog: 1. Enter Name. 2. Click button to pick Base Point. 3. Select objects. Press OK. Replaces original with Block instance.
Export selected geometry or an existing block out to a separate external file.
Type 'I' + Enter. Opens list of blocks in current document. Select one. Click insertion point in viewport.
Define text fields inside blocks that prompt the user for custom values upon insertion (e.g., room numbers, title block data).
Link external drawing files into current document as underlays. Changes to external file update automatically.
Left-to-Right drag: Blue box, selects only objects fully inside. Right-to-Left drag: Green box, selects any object touching the boundary.
Dialog to select objects based on properties (e.g., 'Select all lines on Layer Wall with Color Red').
Select objects, type 'G'. Links them as a single selectable entity without creating a formal block definition.
Switch from Model space to Paper space. Define sheet size, printer margins, and title blocks.
Draw rectangular window in Paper space that looks into Model space. Setup zoom scale (e.g., 1:50) and lock viewport to prevent accidental panning.
Command 'PLOT'. Dialog for PDF/Printer export mapping lineweights (CTB) and selecting plot area. PUBLISH handles batch plotting.
A Cursor just like autocad
Listen for 'Z' + Enter. 'E' = Zoom Extents. Click+Drag = Zoom Window. Bind middle-mouse scroll to Zoom.
Ensure middle-mouse hold+drag universally triggers Pan without interrupting the currently active drawing command.
Force FreeCAD to recompute the document tree and refresh the GUI viewport to clear graphical glitches.
Delete all unused blocks, layers, and linetypes from the document to reduce file size.
Scan selected geometry. Delete duplicate overlapping lines and combine collinear segments.

column5: only with dwg converters, lacks many features
can open/save normally, lacks many features
